content:
# Numbers Chapter 34 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 192-193 | **Source Image:** `NUM_034_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_034_page_002.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0140-pa_0141

---

**1** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**2** "Command the sons of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land of Canaan — this is the land that will fall to you as an inheritance, the land of Canaan according to its boundaries —

**3** your southern side shall extend from the wilderness of Zin along the border of Edom. And your southern boundary on the east shall begin from the end of the Salt Sea.

**4** And your boundary shall turn south of the Ascent of Akrabbim and pass through to Zin, and its limit shall be south of Kadesh-barnea. And it shall go out to Hazar-addar and pass through to Azmon.

**5** And the boundary shall turn from Azmon to the Brook of Egypt, and its limit shall be at the Sea.

**6** And as for the western boundary — the Great Sea shall be your boundary. This shall be your western boundary.

**7** And this shall be your northern boundary: from the Great Sea you shall mark out your line to Mount Hor.

**8** From Mount Hor you shall mark out your line to Lebo-hamath, and the limit of the boundary shall be at Zedad.

**9** And the boundary shall go out to Ziphron, and its limit shall be at Hazar-enan. This shall be your northern boundary.

**10** And you shall mark out your eastern boundary from Hazar-enan to Shepham.

**11** And the boundary shall go down from Shepham to Riblah, on the east side of Ain. And the boundary shall descend and reach the slope on the eastern side of the Sea of Kinnereth.

**12** And the boundary shall go down to the Jordan, and its limit shall be at the Salt Sea. This shall be your land according to its boundaries all around."

**13** And Moses commanded the sons of Israel, saying, "This is the land that you shall receive by lot as an inheritance, which YHWH has commanded to give to the nine tribes and the half-tribe.

**14** For the tribe of the sons of Reuben according to their ancestral households, and the tribe of the sons of Gad according to their ancestral households, have already received their inheritance; and the half-tribe of Manasseh have received their inheritance.

**15** The two tribes and the half-tribe have received their inheritance beyond the Jordan, across from Jericho, eastward toward the sunrise."

**16** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**17** "These are the names of the men who shall apportion the land for you as an inheritance: Eleazar the priest and Joshua son of Nun.

**18** And you shall take one leader from each tribe to apportion the land as an inheritance.

**19** And these are the names of the men: from the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh.

**20** And from the tribe of the sons of Simeon, Shemuel son of Ammihud.

**21** From the tribe of Benjamin, Elidad son of Chislon.

**22** And a leader from the tribe of the sons of Dan, Bukki son of Jogli.

**23** From the sons of Joseph: a leader from the tribe of the sons of Manasseh, Hanniel son of Ephod.

**24** And a leader from the tribe of the sons of Ephraim, Kemuel son of Shiphtan.

**25** And a leader from the tribe of the sons of Zebulun, Elizaphan son of Parnach.

**26** And a leader from the tribe of the sons of Issachar, Paltiel son of Azzan.

**27** And a leader from the tribe of the sons of Asher, Ahihud son of Shelomi.

**28** And a leader from the tribe of the sons of Naphtali, Pedahel son of Ammihud."

**29** These are the ones whom YHWH commanded to distribute the inheritance to the sons of Israel in the land of Canaan.

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 2 — "fall to you as an inheritance" (תִּפֹּל לָכֶם בְּנַחֲלָה):** The Hebrew verb *naphal* ("to fall") reflects the practice of casting lots to determine land distribution. The land does not merely "belong" to Israel — it "falls" to them by divine allotment, reinforcing that YHWH is the ultimate allocator of territory. The Vaticanus text (κληρονομία) similarly captures the inheritance concept.

**Verse 3 — "Salt Sea" (יָם הַמֶּלַח):** This is the Dead Sea. The Hebrew designation refers to its extraordinarily high mineral and salt content. I retain the literal Hebrew name rather than the modern geographical term to preserve the original texture.

**Verse 5 — "Brook of Egypt" (נַחְלָה מִצְרָיִם):** This is the Wadi el-Arish, not the Nile. The Hebrew *nachal* indicates a seasonal watercourse (wadi), not a permanent river (*nahar*). This marks the southwestern extent of the promised territory.

**Verse 6 — "Great Sea" (הַיָּם הַגָּדוֹל):** The Mediterranean Sea. In Hebrew geographical orientation, the sea lies to the west, and thus *yam* ("sea") also means "west" — hence the western boundary is simply "the Sea."

**Verse 7 — "Mount Hor" (הֹר הָהָר):** Not to be confused with the Mount Hor near Kadesh where Aaron died (Numbers 20:22-29). This northern Mount Hor is likely a peak in the Lebanon range, marking the northwestern boundary point.

**Verse 8 — "Lebo-hamath" (לְבוֹא חֲמָת):** Literally "the entrance of Hamath." This phrase marks the ideal northern boundary of the promised land, referenced repeatedly in biblical boundary descriptions (cf. Joshua 13:5; Judges 3:3; 1 Kings 8:65). It likely refers to the Beqa Valley region between the Lebanon and Anti-Lebanon mountains.

**Verse 11 — "Sea of Kinnereth" (יָם כִּנֶּרֶת):** This is the Sea of Galilee. The name *Kinnereth* may derive from *kinnor* ("harp" or "lyre"), possibly reflecting the lake's shape. The Vaticanus reads Χενερεθ.

**Verse 13 — "nine tribes and the half-tribe":** Moses specifies that only nine and a half tribes will receive land west of the Jordan, since Reuben, Gad, and the half-tribe of Manasseh have already been granted territory in Transjordan (see Numbers 32).

**Verses 17-28 — The appointed leaders:** The tribal order here differs from other biblical lists. Notably, Reuben, Gad, and the eastern half of Manasseh are absent (having already received their land). The list begins with Judah and ends with Naphtali, proceeding roughly from south to north — mirroring the geographic order in which the tribes would settle in Canaan. Caleb son of Jephunneh (v. 19) is one of the two faithful spies from Numbers 13-14, now finally approaching the land he was promised.

**Verse 29 — "commanded to distribute" (צִוָּה ... לְנַחֵל):** The chapter closes by reaffirming divine authority over the land distribution. The verb *nachel* ("to give as inheritance") shares its root with *nachalah* ("inheritance"), forming an inclusio with verse 2. The land is not conquered territory to be divided by human preference — it is a divine inheritance allocated by YHWH's designated agents.
